controlRecord.name: CR Dialogue Session Procedure Instance Record

attributes.name: DialogueSessionProcedureInstanceRecordIdentification
attributes.description: A unique reference to an item or an occurrence of Customer Contact Session Procedure

attributes.name: DialogueSessionIdentification
attributes.description:
A unique identifier representing the active service interaction session initiated by Session Dialogue for execution of the selected banking service.
Used to track and coordinate the servicing workflow across subsequent interaction steps and backend service invocations.

A unique identifier assigned to each Dialogue Session to distinguish it from all other sessions. Used for tracking, retrieval, and linking with customer and business activity records.

attributes.name: DialogueSessionStartDate
attributes.description: The exact timestamp when the dialogue session is initiated (e.g., when a call connects, a chat is opened, or a self-service interaction begins).
attributes.relatedBusinessObjectAttrName: Dialogue Session Start Date and Time

attributes.name: DialogueSessionEndDate
attributes.description: The exact timestamp when the dialogue session is concluded (e.g., call ends, chat closed, portal session terminated).
attributes.relatedBusinessObjectAttrName: Dialogue Session End Date and Time

attributes.name: DialogueSessionDuration
attributes.description: The total elapsed time of the session, typically calculated as the difference between End Time and Start Time (may also include idle/hold time if relevant).

attributes.name: DialogueSessionStatus
attributes.description:
The current operational state of the initiated service session.
Indicates whether the servicing workflow has been successfully initiated, is in progress, completed, suspended, or failed.

The current or final state of the dialogue session, such as:
- Active (ongoing session)
- Closed (successfully completed and logged)
- Pending Follow-up (requires additional action, e.g., callback, escalation, or unresolved issue).

attributes.name: DialogueSessionCustomerorContactPartyReference
attributes.description:
Reference to Party who is a specific customer or contact party in session
The verified and trusted identifier of the authenticated customer. Used by downstream service domains to access customer-related products and services.

attributes.name: DialogueSessionAuthenticationStatus
attributes.description:
Indicates whether the customers identity has been successfully verified (e.g., authenticated, partially verified, not verified).

Comment: 
Note that the authentication level may be elevated during the interaction if needed, with the Contact Handler initiating Party Authentication.

attributes.name: DialogueSessionChannelUsed
attributes.description:
The communication medium through which the customer interacts with the enterprise during the session. Examples include voice call, chat, email, social media message, or self-service portal.

Comment:
A contact could include many sessions which could use different channels and devices.

attributes.name: DialogueSessionDeviceUsed
attributes.description:
The hardware or endpoint the customer uses to participate in the session, such as mobile phone, desktop/laptop, tablet, or IVR (interactive voice response) system.

Comment:
A contact could include many sessions which could use different channels and devices.

attributes.name: CustomerContactSessionChannelTransitionsReference
attributes.description:
Reference to the record of any change in the communication channel during a session (e.g., a chat escalated to a phone call), maintaining continuity of the session across multiple channels.

Comment:
A contact consists of one or more sessions. The combination of these sessions is recorded in the contact record, which is then written to the relevant logs, including channel activity, customer event, and servicing event logs

attributes.name: DialogueSessionLog
attributes.description: A complete record of the conversation during the session, including chat messages, call notes, emails, or any other exchanged content. It captures both customer and agent/bot contributions for reference, analysis, and compliance.

attributes.name: DialogueSessionIntentsDetected
attributes.description: The purpose, need, or goal identified from the customer’s inputs during the session. Examples include request for information, complaint, order placement, or troubleshooting. This is typically determined using structured forms, NLP (natural language processing), or agent recognition.

attributes.name: DialogueSessionActions
attributes.description: Tasks executed during the session, such as processing service requests, placing orders, delivering notifications, or updating customer data.

attributes.name: DialogueSessionOutcomes
attributes.description: The results of these actions, including session resolution status (resolved, pending, escalated), customer satisfaction, and any triggers for future interactions.

attributes.name: DialogueSessionAgentReference
attributes.description: Reference to Party (human or even virtual agent/bot) managing the session.

attributes.name: CustomerContactReference
attributes.description:
Reference to the Customer Contact witch launches a Session Dialogue 

Comment: Contact Handler SD is responsible to manage the Customer Contact.

attributes.name: DialogueSessionProcedureInstanceRecordIdentificationIssuerReference
attributes.description: Reference to a Party that assigns the identification.

attributes.name: CustomerProductReference
attributes.description: Reference to the customer product selected or associated with the requested service interaction.

attributes.name: SelectedService
attributes.description: he banking service determined by Contact Handler as the requested and eligible service to be executed for the customer. Defines the servicing workflow that Session Dialogue must orchestrate.

attributes.name: SelectedRouting
attributes.description: The servicing mode determined during interaction evaluation, indicating how the customer interaction should proceed. Examples include self-service, assisted service, or escalation to a servicing representative.

serviceOperations.name: Initiate Dialogue Session Procedure Instance Record
Update Dialogue Session Procedure Instance Record
Capture Dialogue Session Procedure Instance Record
Execute Dialogue Session Procedure Instance Record
Request Dialogue Session Procedure Instance Record
Control Dialogue Session Procedure Instance Record
Exchange Dialogue Session Procedure Instance Record
Retrieve Dialogue Session Procedure Instance Record
Notify Dialogue Session Procedure Instance Record

behaviourQualifiers.name: BQ Product or Service Invocation Instance Record
behaviourQualifiers.description:
This is the step in the Dialouge Session procedure where the session executes actions or retrieves data related to the customers requested products or services.


** General comment **
The Product or Service invocation BQ, having confirmed that the party has the authority and has been authentication will present service access available to a selected product or service. It is assumed that this will include displaying what kind of product services might be available for the customer/party to select from.

attributes.name: ProductorServiceInvocationInstanceRecordIdentification
attributes.description: A unique reference to an item or an occurrence of Product or Service Invocation Workstep

attributes.name: ProductorServiceInvocationProductorServiceReference
attributes.description:
Reference to specific instance of the product/service to operate on.

Directs the action to the product instance
attributes.relatedBusinessObjectAttrName: Product or Service Invocation Product or Service Reference

attributes.name: ProductorServiceInvocationProductorServiceType
attributes.description:
Type of product/service being acted on

Helps the invocation logic select the correct service domain e.g. currentg account, savings account, credit card, loan, investment, etc.
attributes.relatedBusinessObjectAttrName: Product or Service Invocation Product or Service Reference

attributes.name: ProductorServiceInvocationRequestedAction
attributes.description:
Type of action requested

Determines whether to retrieve data, execute transaction, or update profile e.g., Retrieval (balance, statement), transaction (payment, transfer), update (profile, preferences).

** General comment **
We would expect that part of the interaction between the access BQ and the product fulfillment Service Domain would be to present what types of access the customer might be able to use (this would be product specific and might be filtered based on the level of authentication and authorization that the customer has established during the contact )
attributes.relatedBusinessObjectAttrName: Product or Service Invocation Requested Action Type

attributes.name: ProductorServiceInvocationActionParameters
attributes.description:
Input values for the requested action

Provides all necessary details for execution e.g., amount, recipient, date, payment method, or other service-specific inputs.
attributes.relatedBusinessObjectAttrName: Product or Service Invocation Requested Action Reference

attributes.name: DialogueSessionProcedureInstanceRecordIdentification
attributes.description: A unique reference to an item or an occurrence of Dialogue Session Procedure

attributes.name: ProductorServiceInvocationRequestedActionResult
attributes.description:
Result of the requested action

To return structured results to the customer session for display, communication, or next-step decisioning.
attributes.relatedBusinessObjectAttrName: Product or Service Invocation Requested Action Outcome Status

behaviourQualifiers.name: BQ Order Instance Record
behaviourQualifiers.description: A course of action for doing Order Invocation in the context of executing the <CR>

attributes.name: DialogueSessionProcedureInstanceRecordIdentification
attributes.description: Unique identifier of a control record instance

attributes.name: OrderInstanceRecordIdentification
attributes.description: Unique identifier of a behavior qualifier instance

attributes.name: SessionContext
attributes.description:
Continuity of the live customer conversation

Session Context is the complete, time-bound representation of an active customer interaction, capturing the conversational state, historical exchanges, and correlation identifiers that ensure continuity of the dialogue across multiple service invocations.

It represents what is happening right now in the customer conversation, including:
Where the customer is in the journey
What has already been asked/answered
What decisions have been made so far
The identity of the interaction thread

attributes.name: CustomerData
attributes.description:
Identity and personal attributes of the customer

Customer Data is the structured and semi-structured set of personal, demographic, and identifying attributes provided or confirmed during the interaction, used to uniquely identify and assess a natural or legal person.

It represents:

Who the customer is
What we know about them
What they claim their identity is

Typical elements include:
Name
Date of birth
National identifiers (SSN, passport, ID)
Address and contact details

attributes.name: OrderType
attributes.description:
Classification of the structured business request

Order Type is the formal classification of a business request that defines the nature, structure, and lifecycle behavior of the downstream process to be executed by a service domain.

It defines:

What kind of business outcome is being requested

Examples:

CustomerOnboardingOrder
AccountOpeningOrder
CreditApplicationOrder

attributes.name: ChannelContext
attributes.description:
Medium and environment of customer interaction

Channel Context is the representation of the customers interaction medium and delivery environment, capturing how and through which service channel the customer is engaging with the bank.

It answers:

Where and how is the customer interacting with us?

Examples:

Branch
Mobile app
Online banking portal
Call center
API / partner channel

attributes.name: WorkflowIntent
attributes.description:
Desired business outcome from the customers perspective

Workflow Intent is the explicit business objective derived from the customers request and interaction context, representing the desired end-state or outcome the customer expects the organization to deliver.

It captures:

What does the customer want to achieve?

Examples:

Open a new bank account
Become a new customer
Update customer identity details
Request financial onboarding approval

behaviourQualifiers.name: BQ Onboarding Instance Record
behaviourQualifiers.description:
A course of action for doing Onboarding in the context of executing the <CR>

Onboarding is the behavior qualifier responsible for managing the customer interaction associated with establishing a new party relationship with the institution, including the collection, validation, presentation, and submission of information required to initiate and support the customer onboarding process performed by Party Lifecycle Management SD.

attributes.name: DialogueSessionProcedureInstanceRecordIdentification
attributes.description: Unique identifier of a control record instance

attributes.name: OnboardingInstanceRecordIdentification
attributes.description: Unique identifier of a behavior qualifier instance

attributes.name: OnboardingSessionReference
attributes.description: Information used to identify, correlate, and manage the onboarding interaction within the customer dialogue session. It provides the context for tracking the onboarding journey from initiation to completion. Example: Session ID: SD-12345, Onboarding Session ID: ONB-001, Status: Information Gathering, Channel: Mobile Banking

attributes.name: ProspectivePartyInformation
attributes.description: The personal, demographic, and relationship information collected from a prospective customer to establish their identity and support the creation of a party record. Example: Name: John Smith, Date of Birth: 15-May-1988, Nationality: Belgian, Residential Address: 10 Main Street, Brussels

attributes.name: IdentificationInformation
attributes.description: The information describing the identity credentials and supporting documents provided by the prospective customer for identity verification purposes. Example: Passport Number: AB123456, Issuing Country: Belgium, Expiry Date: 15-May-2032

attributes.name: ContactandCommunicationPreferences
attributes.description: The information defining how the prospective customer can be contacted and how they prefer to receive communications from the institution. Example: Email: john.smith@email.com, Mobile: +32xxxxxxx, Preferred Language: English, Preferred Contact Method: Email

attributes.name: OnboardingRequirementsStatus
attributes.description: Information used to monitor the completion and validation status of the information, documents, and declarations required before the onboarding request can be submitted for processing. Example: Identity Documents: Complete, Address Verification: Pending, Submission Ready: No

attributes.name: PLMInvocationInformation
attributes.description: The details associated with the submission of the onboarding request from Session Dialogue SD to Party Lifecycle Management SD, including references used for tracking and correlation. Example: Request ID: PLM-78901, Invocation Date: 04-Jun-2026, Correlation ID: CORR-45678, Status: Submitted

attributes.name: OnboardingOutcomeInformation
attributes.description: The information returned by Party Lifecycle Management SD describing the result of the onboarding process and any resulting party identifiers or next actions. Example: Party ID: P123456, Onboarding Status: Approved, Risk Rating: Low, Next Action: Open Current Account
